memoryArtifacts.headline: Wikipedia Descendants
memoryArtifacts.description:
Descendants

Suffolk's only known legitimate son, John, became the 2nd Duke of Suffolk in 1463.

Suffolk also fathered an illegitimate daughter, Jane de la Pole. Her mother is said to have been a nun, …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Jackanapes
memoryArtifacts.description:
De la Pole's nickname "Jacknapes" comes from "Jack of Naples", a slang term for a monkey at the time. 

The phrase "of Naples" was rendered "a Napes" in vernacular. It probably derives from *Jak a Nap …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Notes individuelles de William de La Pole
memoryArtifacts.description:
Born 16 October 1396 (Sunday) - Cotton, Suffolk, Engand

Deceased 2 May 1450 (Thursday), aged 53 years old

Grand chambellan, amiral

 Il a été décapité ! 




Deceased 2 May 1450 (Thursday), aged 53  …

popularStory.headline: Wikipedia Biography
popularStory.description:
Biography

William de la Pole was born at Cotton, Suffolk, the second son of Michael de la Pole, 2nd Earl of Suffolk, and Katherine de Stafford, daughter of Hugh de Stafford, 2nd Earl of Stafford, KG, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de la Pole II於1396年10月16日出生在英國素福爾可科頓時，他的父親Michael de la Pole II29歲，母親Katherine Stafford22歲。他於大約1430年11月11日與Alyce Chaucer在英國奧可福爾艾尤爾梅結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1450年5月2日在英國肯特多福享年53歲，埋葬在英國越可爾赫爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de la Pole II于1396年10月16日出生在英国素福爾可科頓时，他的父亲Michael de la Pole II29岁，母亲Katherine Stafford22岁。他于大约1430年11月11日与Alyce Chaucer在英国奧可福爾艾尤爾梅结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1450年5月2日在英国肯特多福享年53岁，埋葬在英国越可爾赫爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de la Pole II was born on 16 October 1396, in Cotton, Suffolk, England, his father, Michael de la Pole II, was 29 and his mother, Katherine Stafford, was 22. He married Alyce Chaucer about 11 November 1430, in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 2 May 1450, in Dover, Kent, England, at the age of 53, and was buried in Hull, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de la Pole II est né le 16 octobre 1396, à Cotton, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Michael de la Pole II, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Katherine Stafford, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Alyce Chaucer environ 11 novembre 1430, à Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 2 mai 1450, à Dover, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de la Pole II am 16. Oktober 1396 in Cotton, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Michael de la Pole II 29 und seine Mutter Katherine Stafford 22. Er hat um 11. November 1430 in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England Alyce Chaucer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 2. Mai 1450 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Dover, Kent, England und wurde in Hull, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de la Pole II è nato il il 16 ottobre 1396 nel Cotton, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Michael de la Pole II, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Stafford, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Alyce Chaucer circa 11 novembre 1430, nel Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 2 maggio 1450, nel Dover, Kent, England, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Hull, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de la Pole IIが1396年10月16日にイギリススフォルクコットオンで生まれた時，父Michael de la Pole IIは29歳，母Katherine Staffordは22歳であった。約1430年11月11日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアエウエルメでAlyce Chaucerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1450年5月2日にイギリスケントドバーで53歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアハルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de la Pole II(이)가 1396년 10월 16일 영국 수폴크 컷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Michael de la Pole II(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Stafford(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1430년 11월 11일 영국 스퍼드실르 웰에서 Alyce Chaucer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1450년 5월 2일 영국 켄트 도버에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 훌에 묻혔다.

pt.16 October 1396–2 May 1450: 16 de outubro de 1396–2 de maio de 1450
pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de la Pole II nasceu em 16 de outubro de 1396, em Cotton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Michael de la Pole II, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Stafford, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Alyce Chaucer aproximadamente 11 de novembro de 1430, em Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 2 de maio de 1450, em Dover, Kent, Inglaterra, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de la Pole II родился 16 октября 1396 г. в местности коттон, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Michael de la Pole II было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Stafford, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Alyce Chaucer около 11 ноября 1430 г. в местности ювелм, оксфордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 2 мая 1450 г. в местности довир, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия.

es.16 October 1396–2 May 1450: 16 de octubre de 1396–2 de mayo de 1450
es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de la Pole II nació el 16 de octubre de 1396, en Cotton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Michael de la Pole II, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Katherine Stafford, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Alyce Chaucer aproximadamente 11 de noviembre de 1430, en Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 2 de mayo de 1450, en Dover, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
